meta.title: Meshy Comprehensive User Research Study
research_questions: Who are our users, what are their 3D backgrounds, and what drives their creative work?
How does Meshy fit into users' complete workflow — from ideation to final output?
What are the key pain points, workarounds, and unmet needs in users' 3D creation process?
What features or improvements would create the most value for different user segments?
How does Meshy compare to alternatives in users' specific workflows?

target_user.description: All Meshy users across segments — game developers, 3D printing hobbyists, animators, professional artists, students, and more
segments: Indie Game Developers: need game-ready assets, consistent style, low-poly options
3D Printing Hobbyists: need print-ready models, flat bases, manifold geometry
Animation/Film Creators: need facial fidelity, rigging, lip-sync, animation chaining
Professional 3D Artists / VFX: use Meshy for block-out/concepting, need clean topology
Hobbyists / Explorers: creative exploration, VRChat, digital art
XR/AR/VR Developers: low-poly with strict triangle budgets

meta.generated_by: user-research skill v2 — enriched from 100+ past interview analysis

opening.script: Hi, thank you for taking the time to chat with me today. I'm from the Meshy team. We're doing some research to understand people's experience with our product — there are no right or wrong answers, and I'm genuinely curious about your experience. Everything you share is confidential and will only be used to improve our product. This should take about 15 minutes. Ready to get started?

questions.text: Tell me a little about yourself — what kind of work or projects involve 3D content for you?
probes: Is this for professional work, a side project, or a hobby?
How long have you been working with 3D?
Are you working solo or with a team?
questions.avoid: Do not define the user's identity for them (e.g., 'So you're a game developer')
questions.purpose: Detect user segment and adapt subsequent questions

questions.text: How did you first discover Meshy, and what made you decide to try it?
probes: What were you using before Meshy?
Was there a specific project or need that led you to it?
What were your first impressions when you started using it?
questions.avoid: Do not suggest discovery channels (e.g., 'Did you find us on YouTube?')

questions.text: Walk me through your typical workflow when you use Meshy — from the idea in your head to the final output.
probes: Which Meshy features do you use most?
Do you use other tools together with Meshy? What does each tool handle?
Which stage takes the longest or is the most frustrating?
When the result isn't quite right, what do you do?

game_dev: How do you handle poly count and engine integration?
Do you need style consistency across assets?
printing: Do you need to do post-processing for printability?
Any issues with flat bases or manifold geometry?
animation: Any needs for facial expressions or lip-sync?
artist: How's the topology for downstream sculpting?
Do you use Meshy output as a base mesh?

questions.avoid: Do not assume their workflow — let them describe it

questions.text: Thinking about the 3D models you've generated with Meshy — what works well, and where do you feel the quality could improve?
probes: Can you think of a specific example where the result surprised you — positively or negatively?
How do the results compare to what you had in mind?
Are there types of models that Meshy handles better or worse?
known_areas_to_listen_for: Texture quality: smudgy, painterly, baked lighting
Mesh integrity: floating geometry, extra limbs, artifacts
Prompt reliability: complex prompts vs simple
questions.avoid: Do not presume satisfaction or dissatisfaction

questions.text: What's the biggest challenge or limitation you face when using Meshy for your work?
probes: Has that ever caused you to abandon a project or find a different approach?
How are you handling that limitation right now?
If that issue were resolved, how would it change your workflow?
questions.avoid: Do not suggest pain points — let them surface naturally

questions.text: Have you tried any other AI 3D tools? How does your experience with them compare?
probes: What does each tool do well?
Is there anything another tool does that you wish Meshy could do?
What keeps you coming back to Meshy (or what drove you away)?
questions.avoid: Do not name-drop competitors — let the user bring them up

questions.text: If you could wave a magic wand and add one capability to Meshy, what would make the biggest difference for your work?
probes: What would that enable you to do that you can't do today?
Is there any feature you've seen elsewhere that you'd love Meshy to have?

questions.text: Is there anything else about your experience with Meshy — or 3D creation in general — that we haven't covered but you think is important for us to know?
questions.avoid: Do not steer toward negativity (don't say 'anything else you didn't like?')

closing.script: Thank you so much for sharing your experience. Your feedback is incredibly valuable — it directly helps us make Meshy better for creators like you. Have a wonderful day!

key_pain_points_from_research: Mesh quality: floating geometry, extra limbs, face artifacts, back-side issues
Texture: smudgy/painterly on hard surfaces, baked lighting, style inconsistency
Coarse-refined gap: preview very different from final, credits feel like a gamble
3D printing: no flat base, non-manifold output, no printability check
Animation: auto-rigging fails on non-humanoid, limited creature animation
No in-app local mesh editing (must export to Blender for small fixes)
No asset folders or batch download
No style lock across multiple generations
Prompt reliability: complex prompts sometimes worse than simple ones
top_feature_requests_from_research: In-app local mesh editing (select, delete, remove extra parts)
Style lock / style presets for consistent multi-asset generation
Smart retopology and clean UV maps
